questions.question:
Consider the following processes with their burst times:
P1: 10 ms, P2: 5 ms, P3: 8 ms, P4: 12 ms
All processes arrive at time 0. Calculate the average waiting time and turnaround time using (a) FCFS and (b) SJF (non-preemptive) scheduling algorithms. Show the Gantt chart for both.
keyPoints: FCFS: Execute in order P1→P2→P3→P4, waiting time = sum of burst times of previous processes
SJF: Execute in order of shortest burst time P2(5)→P1(10)→P3(8)→P4(12) after sorting by burst time
Calculate waiting time for each process and then find average
Turnaround time = waiting time + burst time for each process

questions.question:
Explain the Round Robin scheduling algorithm with a time quantum of 4 ms. For the following processes arriving at time 0:
P1: 10 ms, P2: 6 ms, P3: 8 ms
Draw the Gantt chart and calculate average waiting time and turnaround time.
keyPoints: Round Robin uses time quantum (q=4ms), each process gets CPU for q ms in circular order
Waiting time = turnaround time - burst time for each process
Round Robin provides better response time but may have higher average waiting time than SJF

questions.question:
(a) Explain the difference between preemptive and non-preemptive scheduling algorithms with one example of each. (b) Consider processes with arrival times:
P1: BT=7, AT=0, P2: BT=4, AT=1, P3: BT=9, AT=2, P4: BT=5, AT=3
Calculate average waiting time and turnaround time using: (i) FCFS (ii) Non-preemptive SJF (iii) Preemptive SJF (Shortest Remaining Time First). (c) Compare these algorithms in terms of throughput, CPU utilization, and convoy effect.
keyPoints: Non-preemptive: once CPU allocated, process runs to completion (FCFS, SJF non-preemptive); Preemptive: process can be interrupted (Round Robin, SRTF)
For SRTF: at t=0 P1 runs; at t=1 P2 arrives, compare P1(6) vs P2(4), P2 runs; at t=2 P3 arrives, compare P1(6) vs P2(3) vs P3(9), P2 continues
SRTF has minimum average waiting time but may cause starvation; FCFS suffers from convoy effect
Throughput: highest in SRTF; CPU utilization: similar for all in loaded system; Convoy effect: severe in FCFS

questions.question:
(a) Define and explain the Priority Scheduling algorithm (both preemptive and non-preemptive). Discuss its advantages, disadvantages, and the problem of starvation. (b) For the following processes with priorities (lower number = higher priority):
P1: BT=6, Priority=3, AT=0
P2: BT=8, Priority=1, AT=1
P3: BT=7, Priority=4, AT=2
P4: BT=3, Priority=2, AT=3
Calculate waiting time and turnaround time using (i) Non-preemptive Priority (ii) Round Robin with q=4. (c) Explain how aging can be used to solve the starvation problem in priority scheduling.
keyPoints: Priority scheduling: CPU allocated to highest priority process; non-preemptive once started, preemptive if higher priority arrives
Starvation: low priority processes may never get CPU if higher priority processes keep arriving
For non-preemptive priority: execute in order P2→P4→P1→P3 based on priority values
Aging: gradually increase priority of waiting processes over time, so even low priority processes eventually get scheduled
